text:
Was promised a refund of my prepaid account as I transitioned to postpaid. Once I had transferred, they decided they didn't want to honor that refund anymore. I've been sent between billing departments countless times and spent more hours with their support than I thought was possible, to no avail.

They'd rather lose me and my family as monthly customers than see that I received the refund I was promised. Needless to say we will be terminating our service with T-mobile.

I live in a rural area near Effingham Illinois. I was informed on line I would have great service in my area. I bought 2 phones on an installment plan. I cannot use my phones anywhere outside of my house for about 5 miles in any direction. The only reason I can use them in my house is because I have wi-fi and I can use them with it.

<3 Beware of T-Mobile..  I cancelled an account and paid in full.  T-mobile kept sending me a letter saying I over paid $14.55.. I kept waiting for the check.  I called customer service and did what they said to verify myself at a nearby T-Mobile.  Today I did that and was told the they cannot give me a gift card or back to my Visa but only can credit it to my account which is closed.  The  person at T-Moblile trying to help had to spend around an hour talking to at least 4 robots and identify himself and myself at least 3-4 times telling the same stor and verifying.  Now that is a great racket I say..How many customers are scammed by these tactics???  I could not believe T-Mobile would do business in such a manner!  It is disgusting T-Mobile!!  It is not the amount but it is the principle of not doing good business..  THINK about it!!  I am going to post this until I get response in the right course on all 10 social media sites and over 5000 friends following.  Shame on you T-MOBILE!!!!!!

i just got merged from sprint to tmobile they are charging a month before i use my phone and they are adding more fees and late fees because i paid every month on the 13th after calling them over and over to change my billing cycle i basically got ignored this tmobile is very unproffessional i asked them questions they dont even answer i ask them again they ignore the question start talking about something else i spoke with a supervisor named samantha at the alburquerque call center she wouldnt give me her address so fcc could contact them im getting out of this mess dont go with them please

Been without internet connection for 6 hours after a T-Mobile expert shut it off and not one of the other 6 experts I have talked with can fixture out how to turn it back on (?????)

I would and can not recommend t-mobile for any service, phone or internet. 

Sad sad company.
T mobile is the worst service out of all phone services. I dont know how the hell they bought out sprint. Easiest word I can use to describe is they "suck" in phone service.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for years with a few minor issues over the years I was very satisfied, up until the last 3 months. Suddenly about three months ago my service became complete garbage. I have contacted support at least ten times to get this resolved and nothing has happened. I was told the truly unlimited plan with no data limit was no longer supported and it was changed with out my knowledge. I was then told that the new magenta plan was unlimited with no data limit. I am now paying $30 more a month and my service is worse then ever. I will be looking for a new service provider and telling T-Mobile to go take a flying leap!

I have been a customer of T-Mobile for more than 15 years.  They told me that my apartment couldn't bet better than 2mbps speed, even on 5G. Long story short, they lied. I can get over 10mbps through a MVO of thiers. I canceled their phone service, but kept the new wireless internet. Well, after 6 months, it too has slowed to a crawl. I started with 60mbs on average. Sometimes getting 130mbps, but occasionally having it drop to 1mbps for a few minutes in the evening. Now. I'm luck if I can get 20mbps which isn't even Broadband speed. It even stops for a few minutes now and then. Their customer service kept following the same tired scripts and blaming the gateway hardware. Again long story short, the tower is over saturated and they can't handle the demand.

Needless to say, I am shopping for a new internet provider.

I am a former sprint/T-Mobile customer. 
I moved to an area with bad T-Mobile reception . I switched to ATT because of superior reception. I called T-Mobile and cancelled my my service. They said they would send a mailer for my phone. I did not receive a mailer. I never heard from them again, until yesterday when I received a notice from a collection agency saying I owed them $579. I contacted customer service and received no satisfaction. They told me corporate had denied the request for a mailer because it was past their 14 day satisfaction guaranteed period. I have been given the run around by several T-Mobile stores and customer service. I was a loyal Sprint customer for over 20 years. Needless to say that no longer applies. I am not paying the $579 charge and will eat the hit to my credit score. They are not getting their phone back either.

T-Mobile is absolute garbage. Worst carrier I've ever had by far. I was pulled in with what I thought was a good deal and it's been nothing but dropped calls and no internet connections everywhere I go regardless if it says I have 5G or not. Do yourself a favor and pay a little more to go with a more reputable carrier or you'll end up regretting it like I am!!!!

We changed the T-Mobile got the military family discount $90 a month, We then got the home Internet service $30 per month it works better and faster than AT&T it's amazing we will save so much money thank you T-Mobile

Deceptive and Misleading 
T mobile sent me an email as a csstomer regarding their internet device. Going through their verification process, they told me their internet service is avaialble at my address. Someone called me and told me they will send me the device to try for 30 days and decide if I want to keep it. After 10 days of using the device without any problem, I cancelled my services with Spectrum. 30 Days after cancelling my service with spectrum my internet service was on and off with T mobile. I called and they asked me to reboot the device which i did. Each time after rebooting, the device will be fine for about 3 hours then it goes off and says internet not available. 
When I called them to complaint about this repeated pattern, they told me they do not have interent service yet at my address or they may have removed my service becasue the network was jamming. I could not comprehend what they meant, but i decided it was best I go back to Spectrum. 
With all the stress of not having interent and not able to work from home, they had a problem sending me a label to return their device immediately. They told me to wait a month for a label to be generated to send back the device. 
After waiting for a month, I received and email three days ago that states: 
" Returning your equipment is easy.
Ready to return the equipment listed below? Either stop by a participating T-Mobile store and drop it off there to complete your return or follow these three steps to ship your equipment to us.
1.	Print your attached pre-paid shipping label.
2.	Pack the equipment in a secured box and adhere the shipping label.
3.	Ship the equipment by dropping it off at your nearest UPS or UPS Drop Box location". 
I took the device to their store on 5238 De Zavala Rd Ste 111, San Antonio, TX 78249 and was there unattended for almost two hours. The manager truly lacked leadership comprehension. The staff who tried to attend to me needed a label printed and only the manager has the priviledge to print. He told her to print him the label and this girl took more than one hour helping other customers who came after me and careless of my time. The staff then decided to go and help another customer after waiting for the manager indefinitely. When I asked the staff what is going on, he said he is waiting on the manager. I told him i have been here far longer than every customer in the store and deserved to be serve before them. He told me T Monile is doing me a FAVOR to take back a device that they sent to me deceptively and has caused me to be without internet for weeks. A favor? 
WHAT A MISLEADING AND DISRESPECTFUL WAY OF DOING BUSINESS. SHAME T MOBILE. I HAVE BEEN WITH YOU FOR ABOUT 7 YEARS OR MORE, I NEVER SAW THIS COMING.  
EQUALITY IS RESPECT.

I was a customer with Tmobile for 5 years and enjoyed the experience with them. When a deal for another provider couldn't hold me back, my wife and I decided to switch to save money. When the transfer was 'completed', I called tmobile to make sure there wouldn't be any more charges and that the account was closed, to which they confirmed. A month later I received a charge on my card from Tmobile for a regular cellular bill. I called in to get a reimbursement with absolutely no help at all. They told me to enter a tmobile store to get the issue resolved. After an hour in the retail store, the associate not only wouldn't reimburse me for the previous charge but also wanted me to pay an additional $123.00 for next months bill because "I never called in to officially cancel the account." After arguing back and forth, the associate finally credited my account so that (in her words) I would only have to pay $40. Funny. I would 'only' have to pay $40 for a service I no longer had or was using because I had already made the switch to another provider. I even asked her to listen to the recorded call so that she could hear that the 
 associate said there wouldn't be any more charges and that the account would be closed, but she said that she wouldn't listen to it. They are money grabbers. Worst customer service situation ever.....EVER.

When I picked up the Gateway internet device from the store on 9/14/2022, I was told if I return it within 14 days there would be no charge. I returned it 9/26/2022 and again told there would be no charge. I called, as instructed, that day to let t-mobile know I had canceled. About a week later I received a text that there is an extra $31 fee added to my bill. I questioned it online and was informed that yes there is no charge for the device but a charge for the plan, which they extended to the 29th for some reason I don't understand. I wasn't using the plan from the 26th to the 29th. But, again, I was told on two different occasions, the getting and returning of the Gateway that there would be no charge.

Requested new service, bringing in 3 phone lines and internet service. All looked great, however, signal was extremely weak in our area. Spoke to customer service 5 times within the first 4 days of the new service requesting help to increase our 1 bar signal. Second Gateway device sent, also did not work. Cancelled all service, sent back all devices and verified receipt from UPS within 10days of initial service request. Billed a total of $871.+ and was never able to get satisfaction from any customer service at TMobile. Filed FCC complaint. TMobile contacted me finally and told me that I would receive $183.+ back, however, because we received 7 days of service (???) we would be charged for over 400 minutes of data. Really?

I've been with TMobile since 1994...in 2004 I split the accounts and I kept two accounts. On 3/30/2019 I purchased a S21 Ultra which I was sold on the idea that 5g and a better camera would improve my usage. Also multi tasking was user friendly. I am in sales and use my phone for everything, Email, texts, calls, taking pictures and sending them...mind you my Note 9 was doing all these with 4g. They said it would be faster with 5g. Not a chance. That day was my birthday and I spent 8 hours that night trying to set my new phone. Since the Note 9 was 4 g and the S21 5g..there were two different networks and I could not send a verification code to my old phone once I changed the SIM card..They've fix that since..I think.. Then the trouble started. BOTH S21 AND THE S22, WHICH I WAS ASKED TO UPGRADE AND PURCHASE...DID NOT HANDLE MUKTI TASKING. IF I HAD DATA AND TELECOMMUNICATION COMING IN AT THE SAME TIME, THE PHONE WOULD FREEZE. I had to restart the phone and at times lose my data. That suxxed!!!
Then, both phones would erase certain passwords when updating the systems and I would find out when it was time to sign in and I was busy. The application to appraise cars. My banking aps..app... my Nissan aps. I had to stop what I was doing at work and fix it.. Since at times I forgot the passwords and were also erased feom Google..I had to reset the passwords..I lost money in sales on people tired of waiting...With the Pandemic, I had to go to people's home and use my phone to get the information!!!! 
A total cluster.. I gave them a zero on a survey one time and one of the managers called me to reprememend me for doing so...AT WORK!!!!
I RECENTLY GAVE THEM ANOTHER ZERO AND I AM YET TO RECEIVE A CALL TO SETTLE THIS ISISUE..
MIND YOU I have hours of time trouble shooting those two phones because they would not proceed with the next step without it. My watch has an ISP TO GET CALLS..I PAY EXTRA.. 40 YRDS AWAY AND IT DISCONNECTS BECAUSE THEY DON'T HAVE IT CONNECTED.. I WOULD BE ASHAME TO SHOW THIS AS CUSTOMER SERVICE. THEY HAVE GIVEN ME SOME CREDITS, BUT NEVER CLOSE TO WHAT THIS COST ME..
THE ISSUE IS THAT THE ENGINEERS WHO WORKED ON THE DATA AND TELECOMMUNICATIONS WERE SEPARATE AND DID NOT TEST BOTH AT THE SAME TIME.. I EXPLAINED THIS TO THEM AND THEY SAID THEY WOULD INFORM ENGINEERS..6 MONTHS AGO AND REPEATEDLY SINCE...
THE SAME THING HAPPENED WHEN THEY SWITCH FROM 2G TO 3G..THE NETWORKS WERE TOTALY DIFFERENT AND HAS ISSUES COMING INTO THE DEVICE... 8 FREAKEN G4 PHONES LATER THEY FINALLY SAW WHAT I WAS TALKING ABOUT, AS IT WAS HAPPENING IN TEXAS AND PUERTO RICO...
TO THIS DATE NO FIX FORM ME..I FEEL T-MOBILE IS SHOOTING THE FINGER AT ME TELLUNG ME TO GO FM..... I RECORDS OF EVERYTHING...

Yesterday I went to the T-Mobile store at the Eastview Mall in Victor New York. I needed a new phone as my old one had charging issues. Sheshe was the woman who helped me out with my new phone. She didn't try to sell me anything I didn't need and she saved me a lot of money by having me trade my old phone in.  I was in the store for quite a while, but I didn't mind. Sheshe helped me out while working with others that came in. Her and her co-worker Tanya were polite and knowledgeable. Sheshe  was even polite to the irate man who kept coming back in because he didn't know what he was doing but was blaming everything on the woman that had finished her shift and left (personally I would have decked the guy). I have nothing but good things to say, my new phone is great and getting it was nothing less than enjoyable.

I got off work at 4:00pm @4:30 received a text telling me an an authorized user had added a line. I’m the only one on my account I called 611 and reported it wasn’t me and filed a fraud complaint, the expert that I could barely understand put it in and it will be investigated. I was okay with that then 2 hours later they walked back into the store in another state and put another iPhone 14 on my account. I called 611 again and talked with another expert who was helpful but couldn’t do anything and that it would take about 7 days for the fraud department to investigate. I asked if they could freeze the account or anything his answer was he could give me the stores number and I could call them, I stated why would I tip off the store who didn’t do their job and could be in on it and it’s not my job it is a security issue on t-mobiles part. After this is resolved I am leaving T-mobile after years of being a loyal customer.

Worst customer service in the history of modern companies. I’m on the phone for four Days trying to do a simple telephone transfer from a landline to a T-Mobile mobile line and they do not have the capability in their personnel department to figure out how to do a simple telephone transfer. I would rate them is zero what is zero is not on the scale it is less than one. Save your money and go somewhere else.

I have had a great experience with T-Mobile. I have been with them for 2 years now. I can't really understand all these negative reviews. Everytime I call them I get someone on the phone almost immediately (no waiting) and they are super helpful, professional and polite! I am shocked at how good the service is! Also, they keep adding additional "perks"to my account, including free international texting and data usage!! I don't normally write reviews, but I am so pleased with this company! They deserve credit for their good service!

T-mobile is a horrible company- RUN FAST!!!  Some employees are crooks stealing customer information, and the company never holds employees accountable.  When you call customer care, you can expect to wait for over an hour never to get your issue resolved.  It's as if they have no quality assurance team because they are horrible.  They will hang up in your face, put you on hold without your knowledge, and keep you on hold for over an hour with bouts of silence then hearing hold music again.  I would NEVER wish this on my worse enemy.  Tmobile is TRASH!!

I quit my service with T-Mobile in August because they sent me out Sim cards for for phones when I only ordered one they told me to go into the store and they would just give me the phone when I brought in the Sim card then I was told that I would have to pay in the store even though I just paid online now two months later they’ve charged me $852 for four phones I never received even though I switch to Verizon in August don’t ever use them

T-mobile stole my phones. I paid full price for them and they told me to trade them in on new ones but they never gave me anything for them. They were brand new and when I asked them why they took my new phones and gave me nothing they said they were worthless. Why are they charging hundreds of dollars for worthless phones and then stealing them from customers who paid for them?

I bought a new 1000 dl s3 flip and the camera went black ..got new one under warranty ..and screen delaminated ...got no. 3 and screen delaminated! Like an idiot i bought my daughter a g4 flip now there is an led line across the middle!

The worst customer services I ever experience... not sure where are outsourcing your services to but it's absolutely not working .. 
I called today to request further assistance regarding our Business account and I had 5o wait some 35 minutes because whoever answered my call (almost 4 people) said they need to transfer my call to the BUSINESS Department... however my call was ever transferred to the BUSINESS Department... 
Definitey outsourcing is a waste of your ressources and Waste of your client time 
.. 
I am looking up another provider for our 35+ business lines ...
I HAVE ORDERED A PHONE STOLEN BY UPS PHONE  WAS REPORTED STOLEN .AGENT RE ORDEREDD PHONE . PHONE WAS STOLEN BY UPS AGAIN. THIS MAKE 4 STOLE CELL PHONES.HELP ME

I received a message from my carrier that I could switch to t mobile and receive a free device so I did this. I asked t mobile when I switched if I could change my plan to a cheaper one and they said yes but I tried 3 times and still no luck. They still have me at a $55 plan and I have to pay $9 a month for the device that's supposed to be free. Talk about false advertising. Terrible company
Complete garbage. Home internet 5g is complete trash hasn’t worked since we bought. Can’t play online work from home or watch Netflix. Will never recommend this bs to anyone
This company is by far the worst ever!!! They never stick to what they say and are completely dishonest about everything.

I do not advice anybody to go into this company they will finish your money and they will call it restore fee they charged me 400 for 3 phones next month they are trying to charge me 537.26 for the same three phones.  This company is just trying to collect money and go out of business.  0% company

My customer service experience this morning made me thoroughly regret my decision to get my internet service through t mobile. It's not the first negative t mobile customer service experience surrounding my internet service. Pretty sad that I've had multiple negative experiences with the internet tech support department when I'm just barely a month into service.

If there was a way to leave 0 stars I would. Ever since T-Mobile took over sprint, my service is completely unreliable and worse than it was. I was told that the service would be even better after the merge but that was obviously a lie from hades. I can have 4 bars of service with LTE and it will consistently drop calls and can barley use data even though I pay for unlimited data. I can usually only get on the internet when I’m home using my wifi or using whatever wifi is available when I’m out and about. I would never had gotten T-Mobile had they not bought out sprint.

T-Mobile by far has the worst customer service ever. I’ve had few encounters will T-Mobile customer service reps many times, and they have all be horrendous, but today it was even worse. I wanted to return my home Wi-Fi. Online it says you can do it in store. I went to the closest store and they said that I can’t return it. Then I drove to the next store, which was corporate, and they said that they can be returned in store, but I can’t because I didn’t buy it from a corporate store. And I need to go to the store I bought it from. Fair enough. I drove to the same store I purchased it and they said it’s not possible to return in store. I called the number they provided. They accepted cancellation and said that I’ll be email the return shipping label almost instantly. I didn’t receive it. I called back, and they said that the line wasn’t cancelled so they’ll cancel it right now. They did cancel it and said that I’ll get the email in 10 minutes. Called back 20 minutes later and the girl told me that the service will be cancelled in 30 days and this is when I’ll get the email with the return label lol. I said that I need it right now because I want to return it the next day. She said she will manually cancel it and I’ll be getting the email shortly. So I again didn’t get any email. I called back 1 hour later and finally I had a knowledgeable tech on the phone. He cancelled the line and stayed in the phone until I received the email. So 3 stores, 4 calls, and few hours later to do something that takes exactly 17 seconds. Seriously no. This is not acceptable.

I had Sprint for more than ten years without issue, and had better cell service than I had with the other top carriers. After the merger/take over we decided to make the switch to t mobile, thinking it would be better since they acquired Sprint towers plus their existing coverage. Immediately noticed less than optimum cell service, but thought it was due to the 5G crap and just rolled with it thinking it would get better. In the past few weeks things have become worse, and the past week has been horrible. I travel for my job and rely on my cell service for navigation and calls on the go. I still have zero service this evening, and can’t even make calls while connected to Wi-Fi near Toledo, Ohio. I have followed all of their stupid suggestions such as restarting my phone several times. I am through! Will be switching carriers ASAP!! I am giving t mobile 1 out of 5 stars because that is as low as possible on this site, but truly I believe this to be too high. Probably have better service with a pay as you go service like the old trac phone. I do not recommend t mobile at all. If you are considering t mobile…move on. This is sad. Really missing Sprint!

I like many others think this company is completely dishonest. I have no service at work and cannot use Wi-Fi. Not all places of business allow this. I frequently have no service at home. And even though I can use Wi-Fi, which by the way is NOT T-Mobile, I’m still paying for their service. They will not refund for days I have no service. The staff in local T-Mobile stores are nice and friendly but have no answers and are not helpful for this issue. The coverage maps are a lie! Sprint was a MUCH better company. I hate that they sold out…

I bought a plan over the phone at the end of august. I explained that I needed a SIM card by sept 1 because I was leaving for a month on sept 2. I was assured numerous times that a SIM card would arrive by sept 1 morning the latest. 
When a SIM card didn’t arrive by the end of the day on sept 1, I called to cancel my service and ask for a refund of $35 activation fee. After 6 calls to customer service I still haven’t received that $35 back even though each time I was assured that service was cancelled and I would get that fee back. Finally I went to a store and a store associate called customer service on by behalf. A phone rep said that that $35 fee is non refundable. But at least she confirmed that service was cancelled and that I would not be charged anything else. Today I received a letter saying that if I don’t pay $70, my bill will go to collection. 
So not only I never received a sim card by sept 1 as I was promised multiple times, not only I never activated a SIM card or used the service, I also had to spend hours on the phone with customer service and finally had to go to a store trying to cancel my service and get that $35 back. Every time over the phone I was assured I would get that fee back. But now not only they are not returning the fee, they are trying to get $70 more from me for the service I never used. I never even put a SIM card in my phone because it came after I left the country. Complete scam and rip off.

Absolutely the worse service ive ever had.  Service was better when it was sprint and that was horrible. They boast of 99% of customers covered? I must be the 1. Verizon is best however i left becuase customer service so bad. Now i pay for absolutely nothing. Drive a truck and i go all the way across states without any service at all. Absolutely worthless. Rather have service and treated poorly so going back to verizon. 2 cups and a string be better than t mobile!

By the way i had to wait to get home and use my wifi to even write this review because my service that stinkn bad. I will pay whatever it takes to get away from this horrible crappy company!

I was on hold with customer service for over an hour only to be told they couldn't help me. They transferred me to another dept. I was on hold for 20 minutes. Since then have called back 4 times, only be told I would get a call back. Nothing.

If it were possible I would not even give T-Mobil one star. I had Sprint since 2002, and was very happy with the service. Once Sprint merged with T-Mobil the service started going down hill. Calls started dropping occasionally and texts were taking longer to go through. In February of 2022 we had to get new phones due to the 5g being implemented. Now, calls are constantly being dropped and texts are either taking forever to go through or are not going through at all. I consistently get an error code stating T-Mobil network is not available. It's very frustrating as the service will not be available for hours on end. This happens frequently. I never had this issue with Sprint. As soon as my contract is up, GOOD BYE T-MOBIL!!! I have warned all my friends to not choose T-Mobil.

Unfortunately I've been with T-Mobile for over 8 years now and this is my story of recent times:

I've mentioned to quite a few T-Mobile reps over the last month that when I first joined T-Mobile the customer service was absolutely awful with promised calls back that never happened. Then they got better as time went on.

One thing for sure that I've noticed with this company over the last year is they promise to call you back and never do!

I'm still waiting for 2 calls to come back to me from a  tech support rep and a supervisor. Both of these calls go back to September 6th and it its now September 30th.

 I've never had a call back since, even though both  promised to call me. 'Yes promised so whenever somebody at T-Mobile promises to call you back, I'll put a lot of money on the fact that they won't call you and your forgotten about once the phone call ends.'

I called up T-Mobile 3 days ago and a ticket was made up and I was told within 72 hours someone will get back to me and update me on the situation. Not only have I called about this issue 3 times within that time period, I even had a supervisor called Mary who promised she would call me back with results and once again I've never heard anything back from her since. 

I've even texted them on the phone number where they get back to you and once again no one has ever gotten back to me. I'm done, enough is enough it's time to move on.

I would advise anybody out there to stay as far away from T-Mobile as possible.

edits.text:
THIS IS A ZERO STAR REVIEW:

One of my biggest pet peeves is a business that says they're going to call you back at a certain time and they 100% flake on you. On Tuesday 9-6-22 Alex at T-Mobile tech support promised he would phone back at 5:30 p.m. PST the following day. He 100% flaked.

The morning after the phone call that never happened, being Thursday 9-8-22 I asked for supervisor and got Alice. To fast forward in the story she promised to call me a few hours later at 8:40 a.m. PST. As I write this review it's now 5 hours after that and no phone call ever came through from  Alice. 

T-Mobile used to be like this 8 years ago, then they got better and now they're absolutely horrible again with their customer service.

I would suggest finding a better cell phone carrier that has customer agents that care about the customer.
This company gives great deals to new customers but they neglect those who've been with T-Mobile for years.

text: I had next to no phone service for two months because there were two cell towers in the Greeley, CO. area as confirmed by the T-Mobile store at Centerplace in Greeley, and they said T-Mobile would give me a partial refund, but they wouldn't. I switched to Verizon because I could not get any cell service with T-Mobile in my area. T-Mobile is dishonest and crooked, and I would never use them again for my cell phone service. Verizon's service is excellent, and they have much better coverage than T-Mobile, especially in rural areas. T-Mobile lied to me several times, and they are a dishonest and very poor cell phone service.

Very helpful customer service. After going to store on Stapley in McKellips, went to this location on University and Gilbert. Edward was a huge help! Major difference with the customer service between these two locations. I will continue going to this location at University & Gilbert Road in Mesa moving forward!

There is absolutely no reliable service. Calls are constantly dropping and text never go through. I called consumer services 4 times already and their answers are either to restart the phone or take sim card out and reinstall. I have to do this about 10 times a day minimum. I am switching carriers this weekend.

Terrible customer service and coverage. We were forced to do upgrades to 3 out of 5 phones for this great 5G experience. One of the phones was to be a free upgrade, but every month it is billed and I spend 2 hours trying to get it adjusted. Right now, they owe me about 6 months credit and keep telling me that even though I have it in writing that there is no charge, they cannot back credit me. I'm very close to filing a BBB case and just changing to Verizon.
I absolutely hate T Mobile home internet. I get 3 bars on the router and nothing loads. Everything I load leads to a security error. Heck, even McDonald's WiFi is better.

I tried their home internet which was horrible. I have been told by them that I can use the service for a month and if I’m not happy I can return the equipments and the service automatically will be deactivated. I called them at least 5-7 times and each time I had to spend at least 2 hours on the phone to cancel the service. I got a bill again saying that I owed them another $150. When I asked them why those charges are still there I have been told that they can’t help since they don’t have the passcode which I have never created. I got another letter saying if I don’t pay within couple of weeks they’ll send it to the collection. I would never ever use this company again and I don’t recommend to anyone else. If you don’t want to get a crappy service do not sign up with them.

I have been a Tmobile customer since high school and I am now 37 years old. I have not noticed much improvement in service coverage in all this time, which is becoming more and more frustrating because of how expensive smart phones and cellular plans have become. I cannot believe I have to PAY for such terrible coverage and now, horrible customer service.

I called just now to speak to someone about upgrading my phone and adding a line for my son. I did not wait long, only a minute, before I got a call back to speak to customer service.

I was on the phone with the representative for 30 minutes and had to repeatedly ask, "Hello? Are you there?" because she wasn't saying anything! I was enunciating fine and I feel that I explained what I needed very clearly.

"This is what I want. Here's a promotion I heard about. Is it still going on? If not, what promotions are there?"

I shared the specific phone I was interested in getting (the same one for myself and my son), and she kept saying, "Hang on, let me look it up. Hold on, I'm checking something."

CHECKING WHAT FOR THIRTY MINUTES?!

I got exasperated, went online and found the promotions myself. I read them off to her. She had me "Hold, please" for another vapid three minutes and then regurgitated the same deals I had read to her.

What are y'all paying her for then?!

I'm over it. I'm done with Tmobile. Better service and coverage for the price out there.

This carrier sucks! Specially their costumer service! You can’t get a hold of them by phone without waiting hours and their chat is pretty useless. I am trying to pay my bill And the app doesn’t show or let me pay through it, I can’t register for a T-Mobile ID because it’s not recognizing my email (that I signed up with) AND in the guest pay it doesn’t even let me see the amount I’m supposed to pay. This carrier is ridiculous!
Who else has been scammed by this company. They have some a fake customer service dept and their stores won’t help with anything. I’m being billed for an account a don’t have. Stay away!

Doesn’t care about giving away free new iPhones, basically rewarding fraudsters. Someone ordered 3 new iPhones fraudulently with my info (and even email). Confirmation email did not provide any information I could give T-Mobile to cancel the order (like how..?). When the item shipped, I finally got all the information. Firstly called ups to get it intercepted but only T-Mobile could do that. Called T-Mobile to get them to do that but apparently couldn’t figure out how to do. Put me on hold for 2 hours then either timed out of hung up. Congrats to the owner for 3 new FREE iPhones from this place. You scammed them good.

Update: after dozens of calls to the customer support that was useless I went to the store. The store finally helped me reach a resolution but all the did was send me to the fraud department that the phone support should have done the very first time they called. My name was on the account but because it was fraudulent I couldn’t get the necessary pin since my phone wasn’t associated and they just kept saying they couldn’t help me instead of passing me to the fraud department. 5 stars for the store, 0 starts for phone support.

In the spring I transferred my services from Verizon. When I entered my Sim card the service allowed me to keep my current phone number for iMessages only and gave me a different phone number for phone calls and other messages . I called and Inform T Mobile that I wanted to keep the same phone number. They told me it would be changed in 24 hours. 24 hours lead to a week. When I was unable to keep my phone number without problems, I had to go back to my previous carrier. I use my phone for business as well as personal. I had the service for less than one month. I let T Mobile know that I was leaving and returning back to Verizon. I paid one month for service. And about 5 months later I received a bill for almost $200 stating that I owe for the back services and my services are disconnected due to nonpayment. After I contacted customer service at T-Mobile, they told me to wait a week and they will try to resolve the problem. The resolution to the problem was to send me to collections. I am going to pay them , but I hope that you can avoid this mistake that is costing me $300 for one month of service.   This is a scam.

Update:  I contacted T-Mobile through Twitter and I was told that my balance is now 0. I will check my credit report to make sure.

T Mobile Travel is not reliable, we rented a car with them and ended up paying double, our complaints have not been addressed despite having spoken with them on several occasions. We will never use their services again, there is hidden information and manipulation, such as insurance coverage and then they tell you that you did not read the fine print.

OUR SERVICE WOULD NOT WORK AT OUR NEW HOUSE, TMOBILE WORKED ON IT FOR 4 MONTHS. THEY WORKED ON THE PHONES AND TOWERS AND SIM CARDS, BUT NO SERVICE FOR 4 MONTHS. THEY KNEW WE WERE USING TRAC PHONES FROM WALMART THE WHOLE TIME, BUT THEY STILL SENT A $450 BILL FOR THE 4 MONTHS. THEY REFUSED TO WORK WITH US ON THE BILL. THERE IS NO WAY ANYONE WOULD PAY THAT BILL. THEY SENT ME TO COLLECTIONS AND IT CAME OFF AFTER 2 YEARS, NOW THEY PUT IT BACK ON WITH A NEW DATE. ILLEGALLY!!! THEY TAKE ADVANTAGE OF ELDERLY PEOPLE. BEWARE!!!!!!!

Do not except to receive quality service from T-Mobile especially at night time.l Dealing with the call center at night is horrendous. I have been with t-mobile for 7 yrs and there services have become unreliable. The call center has you on hold for 2 hours than when someone do answer their are speaking in Spanish. So rude when you tell you don’t speak Spanish and he or she continued to speak so you hang up and call back to be disappointed again. I  was Transfer to one customer service to another while being on hold for each time and finally the call drop. If you not going to assist people when they call just don’t answer the phone at all. And you would think they would call back !!! No way. It hard to find people who care about carrying out service they’re getting paid to do!!! 😞😳😒

Don't believe any coverage maps!!! The map I was shown in the T-Mobile store AND the map on the "Coverage?Lite" app both showed a very high level of signal strength in my area, and in my exact location. BOGUS!!!  As I left the store to go home, four bars went down to 3,2,1, ZERO!!!  "No service" at my home. The $35 I spent for the first month of service was NON-REFUNDABLE!  The reps in the store could offer no explanation for the false information.

Go to a store.  New customer.  My first order went in to the ether so I had to go thrut he whole process a second time. Ordered 2 phones and 2 lines.  Got one SIM card.  Went to store and they would not sell me a SIM.   Got cut off from hold three times.  Spent more than an hour trying to get this resolved and esim. Spent more than 40 min on the phone with an "expert" who obviously did not understand me or the product.  Just wanted activation and Esim and she asked me the same damn questions over and over.  Am cancelling order and sending back.
I had another carrier for 15 yrs, 4 bars all the time, their cos serv was less then poor. I walked next door to T mobile.  signed up 3 months ago, 2 bars, hardly works. Im so sad...

Internet service is spotty at best and unusable at times.  Trying to cancel is a never ending loop of "go to the store", just to be told"cancel online" just to be given a customer service number just to be told "go to store" took 2 weeks of circles to cancel.  All around horrible experience!

I do not understand all these reviews. Towers are what gives you service. If your location isn't close enough then use Wi-Fi. Even on your job. Just get the code. Geez. And if you want a good quility phone research the phones. Before you haphazardly buy one. T-Mobile is a decent Service exspecially with their prices for the Service. If you need your phone while traveling for business. Then get the high priced Companies that have more towers. And work out the deal with your job to reimburse you. I have had both of the high priced phone companies and they charge you extra all the time. Me I will stick with my T-Mobile any day. And the Costomer Service is excellent.

STAY AWAY!!!! Liars! I bought a pre-paid card to use in a phone that is unlocked and that I have used on T-mobile service before back on August 27th. It worked and then it didn't work. Long story short, I spoke to 4 supervisors given the issue with the sim card not working after I left the store. The 1st supervisor told me the credit would be applied back to my debit card in 3-5 days. Nothing. I called again, the 2nd supervisor verified that it was a valid credit and I should see it in 5 business days. Nothing. The 3rd, same thing. I called again last week (Sept. 16th or 17th) and spoke to the 4th supervisor. She said I should see it in 3 days and that she did not know why the credit was not applied. It's September 28th, 2022. I still don't have the $70.60 back in my account. Avoid T-mobile like the plague. It's ridiculous, especially if you promised me a credit, knowing your service failed me, you verified the credit was due me, but then you don't follow through and return my funds. I want my money back ASAP and I will NEVER use T-mobile again. I would NEVER recommend them to anyone either. Their customer service is horrible and they lie. Despicable!!!!!

Claud was absolutely awesome with helping e I’m new to TMobile and got a bill for $400 my bill plus mid cycle charges, I had no idea this would happen. I’m so glad I got Claud on the phone it was the best customer service I’ve ever received from any customer service rep in any industry. He is hands down what every company should have in a call center. I hope y’all keep him and give him a raise he rectified the situation and I’m so happy

Please whatever you do do not get service with T-mobile I’m going to save you a whole lot of headache and trouble that I’ve gone through.I had my service for seven days the phone never did work properly they sold me a cheap phone also told me the plug the case and a screen protector was free with the phone when I actually looked at the bill they charged me for it. Was traveling and I needed a phone right away because my Verizon phone fell and broke and I wasn’t able to get one until I got to where I was going as soon as I got here I went right to a T-Mobile store to return their phone and get my money back well because I purchased my phone in Arizona and I’m in Georgia they are giving me a hassle and trying to tell me not only are they gonna charge me 499 for this cheap piece of crap Phone also kept the money that I have already paid them and charge me $60 for a restocking fee.told me I would owe the 106.00 for 7 days when I was in the store everyone that came in the store had nothing but problems with their billing like I said go to Verizon AT&T any other service do not use T-Mobile

I went to my T-mobile on Greenwich road wichita Ks.
I was greeted by Rone Smith the manager.  I have been taken care of him before and I thoroughly enjoyed working with him on my account.  Today I went in again and he assisted me again.  He is extremely polite, helpful and knowledgeable.  I would go back to him anytime.  He is a fine representative of his T-mobile franchise and T-mobile.
This is by far the worse company possible. The foreigners just hang up on you all willingly. Are they not monitoring the calls like they say they’re doing? Hate this company.

Horrible, was an existing unlimited home internet customer, tried to sign up for their basic essentials plan they advertise. Was able to get the voice plan but they sneakily switched my unlimited home plan to 100gb lite garbage plan.
The only way customer service told me I can get back is their more expensive Magenta plan, SCAM. Switching back to cable internet for home service, stay away.

Horrible! And horrible! I wish I could give a negative star. Most incompetent service representatives ever. Got onto a new postpaid plan last month and already have to call them every week to fix one or the other thing in my billing. Even to port numbers to new sims is a procedure of headache. They say it’s ported, but then the bars on my phone disappear. App doesn’t work when switched from one account to another and nobody can do anything about it. If they give me a dollar for the amount of time I have spent making them understand their mistakes, I would get free service for months! Very pathetic “expertise”
Which I could give it a zero. Worst customer service ever. And the service /phones are very glitchy.

edits.text: The sales people will say anything to get  a sale. Customer service although very "happy" provide absolutely no help. Management doesn't care either.

text: After been with sprint for over 14 year had no problems. And after T-mobile merged together the serves became trash can’t even used it to check my email.

I bought a new Apple phone with Tmobile and customer service informed me that you couldn't buy Apple insurance they said that Apple Co., won't allow you add their insurance, That's a lie. my point is that Tmobile is lying about insurance coverage of a new Iphone Tmobile wants you to buy their insurance coverage which is $18.00 a month, Tmobile is$10.00 more than Apple Co. You can buy Apple insurance on your own. You have to go to their Apple website or call them to insure your new phone. You have 90 days to insure with Apple Co. And it doesn't matter where you buy your new IPhone. I found out too late to buy the insurance with Apple Co. Good Luck.

My Phone is lost or maybe stolen. I cannot use the GPS tracker to locate it becuase both Google and T-Mobile after logging in ask for me to type in the security code they sent to my cellphone. Wow you guys are really smart! There is not even an option to chat with a specialist until I send the verification that they sent to my phone. Brilliant!

T-Mobile is a bait and switch company. They'll set you up under a discount, yet they advertise that you receive immediate discounts. My ordeal began on Monday September 19th 2022. I spent a total of 5 hours on the phone with them over the course of 3 days trying to set up service with them to begin my new business. I was told by one customer service representative that I would not see my discount until after the first building cycle, and then told by another customer service agent that I would not see my discount until after two billing cycles. To make matters worse they set me up on the discount for first response instead of my company. I spoke to six individuals and within 72 hours and I canceled in less than 24 hours on the 1st day. On 9-21-22 hoping the issue would be fixed, I reopened the account with them giving me promises which turned out to be misleading and empty. They then had the audacity to charge me for 2 days and I never even used the service one single time. I conducted all of my business from another cell phone using another service.

I have been without full functioning of my phone since 9/2. Drop calls and spotty network to the point I can't make calls. I'm not receiving messages nor can I send them out at times. I've been a customer for 18 or 19 yrs and was only given a $75 discount and my service is still not working.  3 heat tickets out. I call every three days to check and no one can tell me when I will be able to enjoy my phone again. No one offered to even discount my bill I had to ask. Tell me how satisfied am I as a customer.

I placed an order for a new line to be transferred from Verizon, so i waited 2 days before checking on the status of the ordered phone. Turns out it was shipped to a completely different state! T-Mobile claims they can not get me a new phone that I have to wait for it to be shipped back to the warehouse, and I have to wait another so many WEEKS! before i can get a new phone... Explain to me why i would proceed to switch services?? And now i just got sent to the main menu, with no answers.. They refuse to refund my money even after acknowledged the mistake.. I have to wait for the phone to show back up in the warehouse. What if i never does! This is the worst customer service i've ever dealt with.
Tmobile Gaithersburg MD is the worst place to visit, zero customer service and all the staffs acted like customers doesn't exist with their interaction.
Terrible customer service, web site never works, poor service quality!

I am a sprint customer but t mobile owns sprint so they should take responsibility too. I paid for a phone they said they had but dont. Tried to get a refund but didn't.  Promised a new phone and refund. Never got both. Spent over 12 hours on hold and findly came to this site. UPDATE they did findly help me purchase a phone YES purchase even though i was promised a free phone im now paying for it for 2 years. Barely a discount. I should have changed.

Well lets see, i paid them $267 for my final bill under my mothers name, and when she later went to set up a phone line for my sister they told her she still owed the $267 and wouldn’t give her service unless she paid it. 
They lied and told me that paying my phone bill will raise my credit score when in fact it does not.
They told me that if i stayed with them long enough and made my payments on time i would be eligible for $0 down upgrades, however after never missing a payment and being with them for 7 years straight they still demand that i pay 50%-60% of the price of the phone plus shipping and full tax to upgrade.
They claim to have the most coverage but unless im in a major city like Houston my reception is spotty at best. 
The 5G is supposed to be faster than 4G LTE but more often than not it is slower than 4G.
So yeah. I’m switching to Verizon. A place where they actually follow through on their promises. Like having your phone bill payments raise your credit score, actually able to upgrade with $0 down or a small fee, and reliable coverage almost anywhere you go.

I began my journey with Tmobile on May 17th. Rochester NY is confined to Spectrum or Frontier internet so I was happy to find an alternative. We were happy with our 5G gateway until June when we started experiencing constant outages. The tech team overseas had tons of apologies (as their script dictated) but no answers. The ticket to fix it never got resolved. They sent me a 2nd tower to see if it was a hardware issue but it turned out that the problem was still there. I cancelled my account on Aug 28th, but no one told me I had to cancel the other gateway as well. On September 9th I got charged 71 dollars. I cancelled the 2nd gateway and was promised a refund. Two American reps told me that the refund was processed. It wasn't. The overseas tech had to tell me to wait 30 days to ask for a refund. She is going to call me on Oct 8th (or forget and make me have to call them again). I have spent more time calling customer care than using the internet. I never want to go through this experience again.

Sprint was great! However after closing my account (since they merged under T-Mobile), I did not remember my pin.

I went into the store to verify myself as I was owed a 15$ credit after closing my line. The store verified, called the number, and said that I can call back and tell them to check the account notes to bypass the pin as the store verified me.

So after all of this happened in front of me at the store (store reps were great), the T-Mobile rep told me over the phone that there are no notes and that she cannot do anything since she can't get into the closed account unless the store calls them to verify me...

So adding insult to injury, I told her to keep the 15$ and hung up as this is the 4th month I've tried to resolve this which comes out to (if I was being paid for doing this) $0.13 a day (=$15&change/4/30). It's not even worth the hassle anymore.

Clearly T-Mobile needs the money more than I do... It's just too bad they'll waste the donation on sending me paper statements until the end of time! LOL what a joke... (negative stars, if I could).

text: This is the worst cell company to have ever. I was over charge for four months and I'm currently trying to get out of a two year contract. Do not under any circumstances use them as your carrier. Rip of artist...

THIS IS MY OPINION .. DO NOT CALL T-MOBILE DURING EVENING OR LATE NIGHT.  THE CALLS ARE DIRECTED TO THE PHILLIPINES/CALL CENTER  AND THEY DO NOT HELP AT ALL.  THE INFO AND ISSUE GETS REPEATED OVER AND OVER AGAIN, AND NOTHING GETS RESOLVED/FRUSTRATION YOU HAVE A BETTER CHANCE DURING THE DAY AT A CALL CENTER IN UNITED STATES.

T Mobile Bought Sprint at which time sent me a new SIM card. My Samsung Note 9 which I had just paid $1000 for just 3 years prior stopped working. So they said they were sending me a new iPhone as an apology because I had no working cell phone for days and lost out on a new job. I was specifically told I didn’t have to pay anything, it was “a taken care of “ Then a week later I received the new phone. Next thing I know they’re trying to charge me for it! My bill was over $800!!! And I still owed more! T Mobile is scamming customers and tricking them into paying for new phones. This is hands down the worst company ever!!! Save yourself hours and days of stress and switch to Verizon. I should have never left Verizon. At least they don’t steal from their customers.

I wish I could give 0 stars I've called in at least 3 times to cancel the wifi Hotspot they sent me. It never worked and they refuse to send me a shipping label to return it. They say I need a pin number, that I never set up. It's hard to return a defective item of thr customer service representative refuses to help you. They continue to show why they have the worst customer service I've ever dealt with.

Your store in West Saint Paul needs supervision, the staff is not motivated and they do not have a sense of urgency, the wait times are unnecessarily excessive long. There are KIDS running this store!!
Generation Z !!!! THEY DO NOT CARE!!!! You all need to do better this is so sad

If I could give a zero I would. Been calling for months for them to unlock my phone that has been paid off and until now. They keep saying they are putting in a request for it to be done and it will be done in 24hrs. But still unlocked. TMobile customer service really SUCKS!!!

I called T-Mobile and asked them to unlock my cell phone so that I could transfer my service to another, more reasonably priced provided.  T-Mobile told me that it would be done in no more than three days, but probably less because I owned them no money.  Three days went by, but the phone was still locked.  Then started a series of phone calls in which I would first have to give all kinds of information about my phone and explain the problem only to be transferred to another agent and then another.  Usually, the third agent would then have me do all kinds of unnecessary things with my phone, none of which resulted it being unlocked.  They would then say to wait a few days and they would call to see whether everything was alright.  It never was unlocked, and they never called.
I spent almost two weeks and many hours on the phone for nothing.  Everything everyone in their office the Philippines said was just lies in their attempt to make me give up and stay with their company.  Finally, I filed a complaint with the FCC and shortly thereafter the phone was unlocked without me having to do anything to my phone.
I then was able was able to switch to another phone company, but that company was unable to get T-Mobile to transfer my phone number.  To expedite things, I let the new phone company give me a new number.
T-Mobile is the most deceitful company I have ever dealt with.

I have been trying to get RID of them since 8 July.  I signed up at a shop, went home.  NO SERVICE (in spite of assurances to the contrary).  Went back a couple hours later, canceled the whole thing.  Never made a single call or text.  Was told it was all fine.  August comes, I get a bill for $171 (with overdue charges!).  Went to the shop, was told it was all taken care of.  Sept I get another bill and a dunning note from a collection agency.  CUSTOMER SERVICE line was in audible and utterly useless.  AVOID THIS COMPANY

I had the T-Mobile high speed 5G home internet for about 4 months in mid 2022.  The speed varied widely between 80-500 Mbps but more often about 200.  However, speeds varied widely and the modem had to be reset about once per week.  I was never able to log into my account via their website or the app because I would never get the "automatic" 6-digit verification code.  Calls to customer service were polite, but useless with one excuse after another.  I wrote to the president of the company and someone from corporate called me with the same excuses as the people at customer service, even blaming G-mail for verification codes I never received.  As soon as Verizon offered their similar home 5G internet in my area, I switched and have had absolutely no problem for the first month with speeds of 250-350 Mbps and only one modem reset.  

The bottom line is that T-Mobile may have taken over Sprint, but with the same poor quality of Sprint and the same lame excuses.
Worst connection ever. If you work with your phone and you choose t mobile be ready to lose a lot of money

Shortly after signing up with tmobile my info was stolen in Ca, i live in Mi. My phone was turned off and a new metro phone was opened at a tmobile store in Ca. Then accessed my coin base account through my info and recieved the confirmation text from coin base to my phone which was now a metro phone. They then cleaned out my coinbase account then went to my bank account associated with coinbase and tried to withdraw funds from that account. Luckily, my bank wsaw this for what it was, fraud, and contacted me. That account was immediately closed. Even though my tmobile account was closed and then opened with another tmobile phone, metro, in 15 minutes. It took me 2.5 weeks to get my phone number back, a number I had for 15 years. Both the rep at the tmobile store here in Mi and I reported this to the tmobile fraud department no less than 5 times. I have never been contacted by tmobile concerning this issue. Back when this all was being sorted out I was offered a $420.00 credit to pay off my iphone from tmobile. They now refuse to honor that offer. I switched all my lines to another carrier except the one that had a phone agreement associated with it. I can not unlock this phone to switch because tmobile will not honor the agreement. Also, when switching all my lines one was suspended and not turned off. They continued to charge me on a monthly basis for this line even though I had cancelled it. Multiple calls to the to mobile "experts" have only resulted in literally hours of wasted time and frustration because they can not or will not read the notes associated with this account to understand what actually is happening. STAY AWAY FROM THIS CARRIER AT ALL COST
